behavior_in_task_description.reason: The instruction.md explicitly names all key literals, variant names, file paths, test case names, format strings, and documentation URLs that the tests assert on, including RequireFrom(Box<ConstantString>), rel.as_str(), .parent().join(rel), createRequire('<path>'), module.html#modulecreaterequirefilename, all 6 test case names, input.mjs mapping, and {entry_name} variable.

no_solution_leakage.reason: The instruction.md explicitly states the exact implementation: 'The exact syntax must be RequireFrom(Box<ConstantString>)', 'Chain .parent().join(rel) to construct the resolve origin', 'Match JsValue::Url(rel, JsValueUrlKind::Relative) pattern', 'Wrap the relative path in RequireFrom(Box::new(rel.clone()))'. These are not descriptions of broken behavior but precise code-level instructions for the fix.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert on exact gold-specific implementation details: 'RequireFrom(Box<ConstantString>)' exact string, 'RequireFrom(rel)' variable name, 'rel.as_str()' method call, 'rel.clone()' expression, 'RequireFrom(Box::new' pattern, 'JsValue::Url(rel' destructuring pattern, 'createRequire(\'' format string. An alternative correct fix using different variable names (e.g., 'path' instead of 'rel') or different internal structure would fail these text-matching tests.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Every fail_to_pass test reads source files as text and uses string matching (e.g., 'assert "RequireFrom" in src', 'assert "rel.as_str()" in src', 'assert "RequireFrom(Box::new" in src'). No test compiles the Rust code, runs subprocesses, or inspects actual behavior. All tests are purely textual assertions.

test_not_tautological.reason: A stub implementation that simply inserts the required text strings into the source files (e.g., appending comments containing 'RequireFrom(Box<ConstantString>)', 'rel.as_str()', etc.) without implementing any actual functionality would pass all tests, since they only check for string presence in file contents.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All six requirements listed in instruction.md (enum variant, handler logic, URL pattern detection, display implementation, test cases, entry name mapping) are each covered by corresponding test functions.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-organized with clear section comments (Gates, Fail-to-pass, Pass-to-pass), descriptive function names, and detailed docstrings explaining what each test verifies.

structured_data_schema.reason: The task involves modifying Rust source code, not producing structured data like JSON/CSV/API/DB output.

no_typos.reason: File paths, variable names, test case names, and commands in the instruction and test files are consistent and correctly spelled.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: The instruction explicitly names exact files (analyzer/mod.rs, references/mod.rs, unit.rs), exact code patterns ('RequireFrom(Box<ConstantString>)', 'rel.as_str()', '.parent().join(rel)'), and exact enum variant names. While the 'Files to Investigate' section is reasonable, the 'Implementation Requirements' section provides the exact code-level solution.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: This is a code-editing task modifying Rust source files, not a configuration editing task.
